intro: Leandra cuneata, also known as "erva-preta" in Portuguese, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to the tropical forests of Brazil. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Leandra cuneata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Leandra cuneata include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems.
